theme_description: Light Content Area with Dark Sidebar (Materialize Style)

profile_avatars.description: Team photo for About page or general usage

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. GLOBAL STYLES: Update 'index.css' to define CSS variables for the new primary color (#7367F0) and background (#F8F7FA). Ensure dark mode support variables are set for the sidebar.

2. LAYOUT REFACTOR: Modify 'Layout.jsx' to implement the 'Dark Sidebar' pattern. The sidebar should use the dark background color (#2F3349). The Header should be 'floating' (detached from top/sides with margin) and have a drop shadow, matching the Materialize template.

3. DASHBOARD REDESIGN: Rewrite 'Dashboard.jsx'. REMOVE the current gradient hero section. Replace it with a clean grid layout. Top row: 4 Stats Cards (Customers, Orders, Items, Expenses) styled with the new card guidelines (white, shadow, no border).
4. STATS WIDGETS: Update the design of stats widgets. They should have a colored square icon container (opacity 15%) on the left or top-right, with the value and label clearly visible.
5. CHARTS & TABLES: Ensure charts use the new color palette (Purple for primary series). Tables should have clean rows, no vertical borders, and use the new text colors.
6. ICONS: Continue using Lucide React but ensure they are sized consistently (usually 20px or 24px) and colored according to the context (Primary Purple for active, Gray for inactive).
7. DATA-TESTID: PRESERVE ALL existing data-testid attributes. This is critical.
8. RESPONSIVENESS: Ensure the sidebar is collapsible on mobile and uses a backdrop overlay.
universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) only where specified (e.g. floating header).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
Use 'sonner' for toasts. Sonner component are located in /app/frontend/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx
